The findings suggest that extreme misclassification would be necessary to eliminate the observed differences, implying that other factors may contribute to the disparity in birthweights.
cited_evidence: At full term, the parameters of the birthweight distributions were robust to gestational age misclassification.
Extreme types of misclassification were required to remove the divergence in median birthweights for Aborigines and whites.
missing_evidence: Specific factors contributing to the birthweight differences beyond gestational age misclassification.
safety_concerns: Misinterpretation of gestational age data could lead to inappropriate clinical decisions regarding maternal and infant health.
